summary: Start M6-ACCESSIBILITY-CORE-FLOWS-001 after M6-LAYERED-AI-001 closed on origin/main. The client_engineer must harden keyboard reachability, focus-visible states, screen-reader labels, non-color status, reduced-motion/status update handling, and viewport overflow behavior for the core M6 Alpha flows without adding telemetry, accounts, Electron IPC, hidden network calls, or client-side authoritative state.

tests_run.result: Task JSON was valid before start.
M6-ACCESSIBILITY-CORE-FLOWS-001 and M6-BATCH-AUTORUN-BALANCE-DASHBOARD-001 were dependency-ready; accessibility selected first by ready order.

decisions: Start M6-ACCESSIBILITY-CORE-FLOWS-001 as the highest-priority READY M6 task after M6-LAYERED-AI-001 closure PR entered origin/main.
Scope remains core-flow Alpha accessibility, not final M7/M8 accessibility completion or localization/platform matrix completion.
No new production dependency is authorized by default; any proposed accessibility dependency must include purpose, maintenance, license, alternatives, and bundle impact before acceptance.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; telemetry, accounts, server/multiplayer, Electron IPC changes, hidden network calls, and client-side authoritative state are excluded.
risks: R3 client work crosses apps/web and shared UI/client packages, so independent qa_reviewer acceptance and Playwright/Storybook/Web gates are required.

Accessibility evidence must be bounded and honest: viewport, keyboard, focus order, non-color state, reduced-motion/status updates, and screen-reader label spot checks without claiming final accessibility completion.
requested_action: Implement accessibility core-flow hardening within allowed paths, run required tests where available, produce evidence, and emit REVIEW handoff routed to qa_reviewer.
